\subsubsection{Statistical Analysis}

% AUTO-GENERATED: regression results
Linear regression of pass rate on quorum:
\begin{equation}
\text{PassRate} = {{QUORUM_INTERCEPT}} - {{QUORUM_SLOPE}} \times \text{Quorum\%} \quad (R^2 = {{QUORUM_R2}})
\end{equation}

The relationship is statistically significant ($p < 0.001$) with a large effect size ($d = {{QUORUM_EFFECT_SIZE}}$).

\subsection{RQ2: DAO Scale Effects}
% ============================================================================

\subsubsection{Findings}

% AUTO-UPDATED: scale analysis
Figure~\ref{fig:scale-participation} shows how participation changes with DAO size.

\begin{figure}[h]
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=0.8\textwidth]{figures/scale_participation}
    \caption{Average voter turnout as a function of DAO size. {{SCALE_FINDING_CAPTION}}}
    \label{fig:scale-participation}
\end{figure}

Key findings:
\begin{itemize}
    \item {{SCALE_FINDING_1}}
    \item {{SCALE_FINDING_2}}
    \item {{SCALE_FINDING_3}}
\end{itemize}

\subsubsection{Power Law Fit}

Participation follows a power-law decay:
\begin{equation}
\text{Turnout}(n) = {{SCALE_COEFFICIENT}} \times n^{-{{SCALE_EXPONENT}}}
\end{equation}

This is consistent with coordination cost theories predicting $\beta \approx 0.3-0.5$.
